mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly lifting equity valuations and producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid three-month decline in the unemployment rate below a 0.5 percentage point threshold signals accelerating labor market recovery, which boosts consumer spending expectations and drives immediate positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 5-day returns in more than 60 percent of triggered instances despite the unemployment rate falling by the specified threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in industrial production and housing starts indicates broad-based economic expansion, which increases corporate revenue visibility and compresses equity risk premiums, yielding positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: The joint signal produces negative 60-day forward returns in more than half of observed instances, or the interaction term loses statistical significance when controlling for FEDFUNDS.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating consumer price inflation during a tightening monetary regime forces the Federal Reserve to maintain restrictive policy, which compresses equity multiples and generates negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns remain positive or statistically indistinguishable from zero despite inflation exceeding the 0.3 percent monthly threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Rising retail sales and food services revenue directly reflect strengthening aggregate demand, which improves near-term corporate profit margins and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 5-day trading window.
failure_condition: Rank IC drops below 0.02 over a 3-year rolling window, or forward returns show no positive correlation with monthly retail sales growth.

mechanism_narrative: A three-month industrial production contraction exceeding 2.0 percent signals supply-side deterioration and weakening business investment, which reduces earnings guidance and produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: SPX generates positive 60-day returns in more than 50 percent of instances following the specified industrial production decline threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in total nonfarm payrolls and real GDP confirms synchronized labor and output expansion, which validates earnings sustainability and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 20-day horizon.
failure_condition: The joint PAYEMS and GDPC1 growth signal yields negative 20-day forward returns in more than 55 percent of historical instances, or the interaction effect vanishes when isolated from FEDFUNDS changes.

mechanism_narrative: A housing market contraction exceeding 10 percent annually during a high-interest-rate environment signals severe credit tightening and wealth destruction, which depresses consumer confidence and generates negative S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns remain positive or statistically neutral despite the specified housing starts decline, or the signal fails to predict returns when FEDFUNDS is below 2.0 percent.
